entries.canonical_question: How do I get to The Valley?
alternative_phrasings: how can I get to the valley
can I reach the valley with a normal car
how do we reach the valley
can we drive to the valley
entries.short_answer: The Valley is reached by Jeep, ATV, or horseback transfer. Accessible year-round.
entries.full_answer: The Valley is reached by Jeep, ATV, or horseback transfer. Accessible year-round. Parking at the designated area with a 1km walk to the village—assistance is available. If you're unsure, reach out and we can arrange the best option for you.
entries.upsell_hint: Horseback transfer is one of the options—very memorable.

entries.canonical_question: How do I get to The Cabin? Do I need a 4x4?
alternative_phrasings: how can I get to the cabin
can I reach with a normal car
can transport be arranged from the village
entries.short_answer: 3km rugged trail. SUV/Jeep recommended. Normal car can sometimes make it but weather matters.
entries.full_answer: It's an off-road place, so I always like to set expectations clearly. A normal car can sometimes make it, but the road is rough and weather makes a big difference. If you want the stress-free option, I can usually help arrange transport for the last part. If you tell me what car you have and when you're arriving, I can guide you properly.

entries.canonical_question: How do I get there? Can a normal car reach it?
alternative_phrasings: can a normal car reach it
entries.short_answer: Access depends on which property. The Cabin: 3km rugged trail, SUV recommended. The Valley: Jeep, ATV, or horse transfer.
entries.full_answer: Access depends on which property you're looking at. The Cabin has a 3km rugged trail—SUV/Jeep recommended. The Valley uses Jeep, ATV, or horse transfer. If you tell me which one interests you and what car you have, I can guide you properly.

entries.canonical_question: Can a normal car reach The Cabin?
alternative_phrasings: can a normal car reach it
can I reach with a normal car
do I need 4x4 for the cabin
can we drive with a normal car
can a normal car reach the cabin
entries.short_answer: Yes. A normal car can usually reach The Cabin in good conditions. In snow, mud, or heavy rain that can change. Message us on WhatsApp before arrival for the latest road status.
entries.full_answer: Yes. A normal car can usually reach The Cabin in good conditions. In snow, mud, or heavy rain that can change, and arriving before dark is strongly recommended. Message us on WhatsApp before arrival if you want the latest road status.

entries.canonical_question: Can a normal car reach The Valley?
alternative_phrasings: can a normal car reach the valley
entries.short_answer: No. The Valley is reached by Jeep, ATV, or horseback transfer—not by driving yourself. Parking at the designated area, then 1km walk. We can arrange transfer.
entries.full_answer: No. The Valley is reached by Jeep, ATV, or horseback transfer—you don't drive there yourself. Parking at the designated area with a 1km walk to the village. Message us to arrange the best option for your dates.

entries.canonical_question: Do I need a 4x4?
entries.short_answer: The Cabin: SUV/4x4 recommended but not always required. The Valley: no—transfer by Jeep, ATV, or horse. Message us with your car type and dates for a straight answer.
entries.full_answer: The Cabin: SUV or 4x4 is recommended. A normal car can sometimes make it in good weather, but the road is rough. The Valley: you don't drive—transfer by Jeep, ATV, or horse. Message us with your car and dates for the latest.

entries.canonical_question: Can we arrive after dark? Is late arrival possible?
entries.short_answer: The Cabin: strongly recommend arriving before dark—the last part is very different in daylight. The Valley: more flexible. Message us your planned arrival time.
entries.full_answer: The Cabin: I strongly recommend arriving before dark. The last part of the road feels very different in daylight. The Valley: more flexible. If you must arrive late, message us and we'll send clear instructions. Self check-in is possible.

entries.canonical_question: Google Maps sends us wrong. Where do we go?
entries.short_answer: Don't rely on Google Maps alone. We send detailed instructions and GPS coordinates after booking. Save them—you may lose signal approaching.
entries.full_answer: Don't rely on Google Maps alone—it can send you wrong. We send detailed instructions and GPS coordinates after booking. Save or print them before you leave; you may lose phone signal approaching. Message us if anything is unclear.

entries.canonical_question: Is there a hot tub at The Valley?
entries.short_answer: No. The hot tub is at The Cabin. The Valley has showers, ATV, and horse riding.
entries.full_answer: No. The hot tub is at The Cabin. The Valley has full hot water showers, ATV transfer, and horse riding. Different experience—message us to arrange Valley activities.

entries.canonical_question: Is there Wi-Fi or phone signal?
entries.short_answer: The Cabin: no WiFi, weak or no phone signal. The Valley: Starlink in Stone House (~150 Mbps). If you need to work remotely, The Valley is the fit.
entries.full_answer: The Cabin: no WiFi—fully off-grid. Phone signal is weak or absent. The Valley: Starlink in the Stone House, around 150 Mbps, reliable for video calls. If connectivity matters, The Valley is the better fit. Save directions before you lose signal.

entries.canonical_question: Is there electricity? Can I charge my phone?
entries.short_answer: Off-grid: solar and gas. Limited power for essentials.
entries.full_answer: It's off-grid: solar and gas. Limited power for essentials—lights, charging basics. No hair dryers or high-draw appliances. Come prepared to disconnect.

entries.canonical_question: Is there hot water? How do I shower?
entries.short_answer: The Cabin: no running water. Heat water on the stove for bathing. The Valley: full hot water in all units.
entries.full_answer: The Cabin: no running water. Heat water on the stove for bathing—basin system, like camping but more comfortable. The Valley: fresh drinking water on-site, hot water in all units. Bring what you need for your routine.

entries.canonical_question: What kind of toilet? Is there a toilet?
entries.short_answer: Yes. The Cabin: composting toilet inside. The Valley: standard bathrooms.
entries.full_answer: Yes. The Cabin has a composting toilet inside. Simple to use: do your thing and cover with compost. No smell, very hygienic. The Valley has standard bathrooms.

entries.canonical_question: Is the hot tub included in the price?
alternative_phrasings: is hot tub included in the price
do we pay for the hot tub
do we pay extra for hot tub
is the hot tub part of the price
do we need to pay for the hot tub
can i use the hot tub
can we use the hot tub
can we use the hottub in the cabin
can i use the hottub in the cabin
there is an extra charge right
entries.short_answer: Yes. At The Cabin the hot tub itself is included. Wood is extra—{{hotTubWoodBgn}} BGN when we provide it. Let us know in advance if you want to use it.
entries.full_answer: Yes. At The Cabin the hot tub itself is included. Wood is extra and costs {{hotTubWoodBgn}} BGN when we provide it. If you want to use it, let us know in advance so we can guide you—it takes 6–8 hours to heat.

entries.canonical_question: How much does hot tub wood cost? Is there an extra charge for firewood?
alternative_phrasings: how much does extra wood cost
do we pay extra for the hot tub wood
do we need to pay for wood
do we pay for the hot tub
there is an extra charge right
entries.short_answer: No—hot tub wood is extra. At The Cabin: {{hotTubWoodBgn}} BGN when we provide it. Cabin heating firewood is included.
entries.full_answer: No—hot tub wood is extra. At The Cabin it costs {{hotTubWoodBgn}} BGN when we provide it. Cabin heating firewood is included in the booking. Let us know in advance if you want to use the tub.

entries.canonical_question: How long does it take to heat the hot tub?
alternative_phrasings: how long does it take to heat
how long to heat the hot tub
how long does the tub take
how long to warm up the tub
entries.short_answer: At The Cabin: 6–8 hours. Start very early in the morning.
entries.full_answer: At The Cabin the wood-fired hot tub takes 6–8 hours to heat from cold. Start very early—think morning if you want it ready by evening. You maintain the fire yourself. In winter it can take longer or be impractical.

entries.canonical_question: Does the hot tub work in winter?
alternative_phrasings: can we use it in winter
does the tub work when cold
how does the tub work in winter
entries.short_answer: At The Cabin: sometimes. Frozen water and cold make it harder—takes longer, more wood, not always realistic.
entries.full_answer: At The Cabin in winter it's trickier. Frozen water, cold temps—heating takes longer and uses much more wood. Many guests find it impractical. If the hot tub matters for your dates, tell me when you're coming and I'll be straight about what's possible.

entries.canonical_question: How does the hot tub work? Do guests heat it themselves?
alternative_phrasings: how does the hot tub work
do we need to heat it
entries.short_answer: At The Cabin: wood-fired. You light and maintain the fire. Takes 6–8 hours.
entries.full_answer: At The Cabin it's wood-fired—you light and maintain the fire yourself. There's a fireplace inside the tub. Takes 6–8 hours to heat. Water comes from the creek—clean but expect some leaves, it's outdoors.

entries.canonical_question: Is firewood included? Is there an extra charge for firewood?
alternative_phrasings: is there extra charge for firewood
there is an extra charge right
entries.short_answer: Yes. At The Cabin heating firewood is included. Hot tub wood is extra ({{hotTubWoodBgn}} BGN when we provide it).
entries.full_answer: Yes. At The Cabin heating firewood is included—use as much as you need for the fireplace and gas heater. Hot tub wood is separate and costs {{hotTubWoodBgn}} BGN when we provide it.

entries.canonical_question: Will it be warm enough? Is heating inside included?
entries.short_answer: The Cabin: fireplace + gas heater, firewood included. The Valley: modern heating.
entries.full_answer: The Cabin: fireplace and gas heater, firewood included. Cozy and romantic, not luxury central heating. The Valley: all units have modern heating, reliable in all seasons.

entries.canonical_question: Is there a kitchen? Can we cook? Is there a fridge?
alternative_phrasings: how far is the nearest shop
entries.short_answer: The Cabin: basic kitchen with gas and BBQ, no fridge. The Valley: kitchen facilities. Do your shopping before—no shop on-site.
entries.full_answer: The Cabin has a basic kitchen with gas and BBQ. No oven, no classic fridge—cool box and simple groceries work best. The Valley has kitchen facilities in the units. Do your shopping before heading up; the nearest shop is a drive away.

entries.canonical_question: Where is the nearest shop? Should we bring food?
alternative_phrasings: how far is the nearest shop
entries.short_answer: Yes—bring food. The nearest shop is a drive away. Do your shopping before you arrive; once you're there, the point is to stay in nature.
entries.full_answer: Yes—bring food. The nearest shop is a drive away. Do your shopping before you arrive. Once you're there the point is to stay in nature. The Cabin has no fridge, so cool box and simple groceries work best. The Valley has kitchen facilities.

entries.canonical_question: Is it self check-in? Can we arrive late? Arrive before dark?
entries.short_answer: Self check-in possible. Strongly recommend arriving before dark for The Cabin.
entries.full_answer: Self check-in is possible. For The Cabin, I strongly recommend arriving before dark—the last part feels very different in daylight. I send all guests a guide and key instructions before arrival.

entries.canonical_question: Can we bring our dog? Are pets allowed?
entries.short_answer: No. Pets are not permitted at The Cabin. The Valley: check with us—some units may allow. The remote location and wildlife make it unsuitable for pets at The Cabin.
entries.full_answer: No pets at The Cabin—the remote location and wildlife make it unsuitable. The Valley: some units may allow pets; message us to check. If you have a specific situation, we'll tell you honestly.

entries.canonical_question: Can we bring kids? Is it suitable for a baby?
entries.short_answer: The Cabin sleeps 2—best for couples. The Valley has units for families. Off-grid means no running water at The Cabin; consider if that works for young kids.
entries.full_answer: The Cabin sleeps 2 and is best for couples. The Valley has units that work for families. The Cabin is off-grid—no running water, composting toilet. Consider whether that fits with young kids. Message us with your situation and we'll be straight.

entries.canonical_question: Can more people fit? Is it available?
entries.short_answer: The Cabin sleeps 2. The Valley has 13 A-frames and the Stone House. Check the booking calendar.
entries.full_answer: The Cabin sleeps 2 guests. The Valley has 13 A-frames and the Stone House. Check the booking calendar for availability. For extra guests or specific needs, message us.

entries.canonical_question: Can we reschedule? Can we get a refund? Discount?
entries.short_answer: Rescheduling is usually easier than cancellation. Reach out with your situation.
entries.full_answer: I always try to be fair. In most cases the easiest solution is to reschedule rather than cancel, especially if the issue is weather, timing, or not feeling prepared for off-grid. Send me the situation clearly and I'll tell you the fairest option I can offer.

entries.canonical_question: Can we do horse riding? What can we do nearby?
entries.short_answer: Horse riding can be arranged. The nature is the real luxury.
entries.full_answer: Horse riding can be arranged at The Cabin—around 100 BGN per person per hour including gear and guide. The Valley has horseback transfer as an option. The nature is the real luxury—hiking, views, privacy. Very romantic for couples.

entries.canonical_question: What should I expect? Is it like camping?
entries.short_answer: Real off-grid: limited power, no running water, composting toilet. Nature and disconnection.
entries.full_answer: This is a real off-grid stay. Limited power, no running water, composting toilet. If you're looking to disconnect and enjoy nature, it's a beautiful experience. If you want full comfort, The Valley might be a better fit.
